proved: An internal Rust task retrieves the matching provider secret through the credential-vault trait and executes the existing bounded adapter against a loopback fake server.

The task returns provider-neutral normalized output and authors a content-free provider-run record with source IDs, hashes, disclosure, endpoint, policy, retention state, terminal status, usage, and null unknown cost.
The serialized outcome contains neither the credential canary, prompt text, nor disclosure category text.
Disclosure denial returns a typed failed record and does not contact the unreachable loopback endpoint.
Provider IDs serialize exactly as the public descriptor IDs: openai, anthropic, gemini, and xai.
Credential set/status/delete are registered only through typed tauri.ts wrappers and no command returns a credential.
Generation and cancellation are absent from the Tauri handler, and MCP reports fake-network-certified-unwired rather than available.
notProved: A product UI can start a remote model call.
Human disclosure approval is bound to provider, content, and call lifetime by a native surface.
A real provider credential or external network request works.
Streaming or tool calls pass through the task runtime.
Rust-authored records pass the TypeScript semantic validator across the Tauri serialization boundary.
Transient key-entry DOM/memory, macOS/Linux credential stores, logs, exports, or crash artifacts are leak-free.
